prompt:
Write a file 'code.py' with this EXACT content (preserve all whitespace exactly as shown, including tabs):
def main():
	print('hello')
	if True:
		print('world')
	return 0

Now try to edit the file, replacing the text "    print('world')" (4 spaces before print) with "    print('universe')". This edit will FAIL because the actual file uses tabs, not spaces. After the failed edit, read the file to see the actual whitespace, then retry the edit using the correct tab-based old text '\t\tprint(\'world\')'. Verify the final file content.
rationale: Tests boundary condition — whitespace mismatch in edit_file old_text causes silent failure, agent must diagnose and retry
